---
title: "Yang-Mills Lattice Gauge Theory - William's Explorations"

bibliography: references.bib

# format:
#   html:
#     code-fold: true
# jupyter: python3
---

### April 9, 2026

Consider section 1.4 in @chatterjee2026.
 
- Question: Can you write down a formula
for the leading order term of the free energy using
techniques from @Chatterjee2016?

Work on this question especially when my 
log-gases talk is over. (That is, in May).
Quickly resolving this question would
mean solving it by mid-May, after a couple
of weeks of working on it.

Outline until September 1, 2026. 

1. Go through Section 11 and Section 17 of @Chatterjee2016 
step by step by the end of April.
2. As soon as this is finished, think about how 
to summarize the paper as an introduction section 
to my thesis. Work on this in May.
3. The rest of the thesis
will just be filling in the details of @Chatterjee2016's 
paper in my own voice. Work
on this in May, June, and July.

Thus, the thesis should be finished by the end of July,
more or less, with August to spare.

Also for April: prepare my log-gases talk on May 7th.

July will be busy with the CQFT exam preparation.
April, May, and June will also require some work with
the CQFT course, but less work than the seminar.
